_notes: SPEC_X2 mock-machinery fixture (authored oracle — NOT evidence; the real run uses an out-of-weights world-scored corpus, task #13). The recurring question; answered by core-wifi. Shared record store across the sequence (core.json x3 then recurrence.json): core-wifi (answers this), distractor-inventory (expensive, irrelevant to both questions -> never offered -> pruned by B and C for cost savings), backup-addr (answers the recurrence question only). With top_k=1, only core-wifi is offered here, so B and C prune the other two on disuse. B never rematerializes -> it over-prunes backup-addr and fails the recurrence; C rematerializes backup-addr when it becomes relevant -> matches quality at lower cost (distractor stays pruned throughout).
question: What is the lab workshop wifi password? Reply with only the password.

records.text: The lab workshop wifi password is tangerine-flux-9041.

Archival inventory note: the 2019 equipment audit catalogued three oscilloscopes, a spectrum analyzer, two reflow soldering stations, a logic analyzer, and assorted coaxial cabling under asset group D-7, later cross-referenced against the facilities depreciation schedule, the quarterly maintenance rota, and the offsite storage manifest.
The backup server address is 10.0.4.12 on the east rack.
